name: hESC-derived RPE
summary: hESC-derived RPE is a retinal pigment epithelial cell replacement product normalized for early dry AMD and Stargardt disease graft evidence.
mechanism_summary: Uses human embryonic stem-cell-derived RPE transplantation to replace diseased retinal support cells, with evidence bounded to ocular disease outcomes.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: hESC-derived RPE advanced dry AMD and Stargardt phase 1/2 studies; hESC-derived RPE showed graft survival and visual signals in retinal disease.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
